text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development, where developers examine each other's code before it is merged into the main project. The main goal is to catch bugs early, before they reach production and cause problems for users. A fresh pair of eyes can spot issues that the original author might have missed, saving time and effort later. Code reviews also promote knowledge sharing within the team, as developers learn new techniques and understand different parts of the codebase. This collaboration improves overall team quality, ensuring that standards are maintained and everyone contributes to producing reliable, maintainable software.
